quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of career anxiety moved into the board this morning, bringing scattered showers of job woes. Anon's concerns about career tension, education, and parental pressure formed a dense fog that lingered throughout the day.

A brief but intense downpour of fantasies about rich lives swept through the Discourse section, with Anon imagining lives of luxury and suave sophistication.

Meanwhile, a steady drizzle of research into anti-Indian and anti-Hindu sentiment continued, with some Anons claiming to have uncovered evidence of bias.

In the Notices section, a cold snap of financial dependency settled over one Anon's plans for a taxi business, financed entirely by his parents.

Nearby, a single post about rejection by a sex worker brought a brief, sharp gust of frustration and disappointment.

articles.title: Yaar, Career Ki Badi Tension Hai: A Thread on Job Woes
articles.body:
Anon posted on the board, expressing frustration with their current situation: 'kaise ho yaar,career ki badi tension hai uper se maa baap ki roz ki chik chik aur baap ke taaane kya kara jaaye yaaro,24 ka ho gaya,24 mai graduate hua btech cse se job lagi nhi uper se maa baap ke kalesh mai padhai bhi nahi ho paati kiya to kya kara jaaye,3 mhine ki ek unpaid internship kari thi jo convert nhi hui ab fir se ghar mai hoon koi samaadhan koi nivaran kuch suggestion hai to de do'.

Another anon responded with some context, citing a report that 40% of graduates are unemployed and can't find a job after a year, adding that this situation is 'somehow normal.' However, they noted that they don't see many unemployed people in their own surroundings, suggesting that the statistics might not reflect their personal experience.

One respondent asked Anon for more details about their profile, such as age, caste, qualifications, and skill set, in order to better understand their situation and offer advice.

Anon provided some information about their skills, including experience with AI/ML, Python, Linux, and AWS buckets. Some anons offered suggestions, such as applying for government exams or trying to get more internships to gain experience.

However, others expressed skepticism about these options, with one anon commenting that preparing for government exams might 'ruin his life.' The conversation also turned to the topic of income and financial stability, with one anon noting that it's difficult to do anything on a salary of 25k except 'jerkin off to fatkeetas on internet.' Others shared their own struggles with finding employment, with one anon saying they're 'struggling to get a 15k office receptionist job.' Throughout the thread, anons shared their own experiences and offered advice, but also expressed frustration and cynicism about the job market.

As one anon put it, 'Whyd jeets had to breed this much, too much competition here. Níggas are ready to work for peanuts here.'

articles.title: Anon Fantasies Rich Lives
articles.body:
Anon claimed there is a rich person among users of this board, possibly a son of a writer, artist, or musician, who leads an ultra-smooth life. This person supposedly attends high-class events in Delhi and has relationships with wealthy individuals.

Another anon responded with a scenario involving a young man, allegedly the son of a famous composer and musician, who is out with a rich girl in New Delhi. This anon described the pair entering a hotel and socializing.

A third anon chimed in, saying people might think they're role-playing, but claimed such suave, successful individuals do exist.

This anon described a scene at La Galleria in New Delhi, where a tall, young man is apparently wooing the daughters of wealthy people.

One respondent called out the original poster, saying their fantasies are "poison" and waste people's time, and that reality is more meaningful than such fantasies. Another anon stated that Indiachan should not be an incel club for losers.

articles.title: Researching Anti-Indian and Anti-Hindu Sentiment
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion by stating that when you research, 'they get caught' and 'its always them.' One respondent claimed that their research on anti-Indian and anti-Hindu sentiment often connects to Jewish individuals, though they acknowledged that not all Jewish people are involved and that Christians and Muslims are also implicated.

This anon described Jewish involvement as 'just as bad' as that of Christians and Muslims. Another poster reacted with hostility towards Jewish people, suggesting that Indian freedom fighters should have allied with Hitler.

This anon advocated for violence against Jewish people, saying 'nuke all kikes.' A respondent mocked the intelligence of those who might defend Jewish people, calling them 'Lindu bhackcucks.' One brief comment read 'Oy vey shut it down its a mere coincidence,' possibly questioning the validity of a claim or suggesting an alternative perspective.

The discussion remained largely critical of Jewish people, with no countervailing views presented in the thread. The conversation did not explore the original poster's claim about 'them' getting caught in research beyond these exchanges.

articles.title: Anon plans taxi business with parent-financed car
articles.body:
Anon announced they had finalized a car for taxi purposes, with their parents covering the full cost. All earnings from the venture, estimated around 70k, would go towards savings.

One respondent suggested Anon would struggle to repay their parents and recommended a side hustle. Another Anon mentioned VinFast's upcoming taxi service.

Anon responded to queries about their plans, confirming they would use the car for taxi work and seeking advice on the venture.

articles.title: Randi rejection
articles.body: Anon claimed to have been rejected by a sex worker. The original poster expressed frustration, using profanity. Another anon asked how much money the original poster was willing to pay.
